pattern: Membership in a hash set lets you decide if a value has appeared before in constant time, so a single pass detects a repeat as soon as one is inserted twice.
hints: You only need to know whether any value appears more than once, not where or how many times.
Sorting groups equal values next to each other, so a duplicate becomes an adjacent equal pair; alternatively track what you have already seen.
Walk the array adding each value to a set, and return true the instant you try to add a value the set already holds; if the loop ends, return false.

edge_inputs.description: a single element can never repeat
an early duplicate of the first value
duplicate is the very last element matching an earlier one
negative and positive values with a repeated negative

stress.description: large array of random values that may or may not contain a duplicate
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: duplicates arise rarely by chance over a wide range, exercising the false branch

stress.description: large array constructed to contain exactly one duplicated value
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    nums[-1] = nums[0]
    return [nums]
stress.note: guarantees the true branch is exercised at scale
